world.title: Markwayne Mullin confirmed as Department of Homeland Security head
world.description: The US senator will now oversee the embattled department after a series of immigration enforcement blunders.

world.title: Albanese risks losing the voters who put him there
world.description: From foreign policy to domestic reform, Labor risks alienating key supporters and wasting a moment of political opportunity in a volatile landscape.

world.title: Trump Eyes ‘regime Change’ In Iran
world.description: President Donald Trump said Monday that “regime change” was underway in Iran as the United States holds peace talks with an unidentified alternative leader.

world.title: Iranian terror group 'claims responsibility' for attack on Jewish ambulances in London
world.description: An Iranian-aligned terror group is claiming responsibility for an arson attack on Jewish ambulances in London, according to an intelligence website.

world.title: Senate confirms Trump loyalist Markwayne Mullin as homeland security secretary
world.description: Oklahoma senator, confirmed in 54-45 vote, replaces Kristi Noem to lead president’s immigration crackdown

world.title: YouTuber out of Cambodian jail into Taiwan lockup
world.description: A YOUTUBER known as "Goodnight Chicken" returned to Taiwan after spending over two years in a Cambodian prison only to be arrested by Taiwanese authorities upon his arrival at the airport, Oriental Daily reported.

world.title: US-Israeli war on Iran LIVE: Trump says postponing Iran power plant strikes for five days after 'very good' talks
world.description: U.S.-Israeli war on Iran LIVE: Iran faces deadline of US President Donald Trump warning to open Strait of Hormuz. Follow The Hindu for more LIVE updates on March 23 2026 on Iran-Israel war.

world.title: Record beef production expected as national herd remains above 30 million in 2026: MLA Cattle Industry Projections 2026
world.description: Australia is on track for another year of historically high cattle throughput, with Meat & Livestock Australia’s (MLA) latest Australian Cattle Industry Projections forecasting total slaughter to reach 9.45 million head in 2026, the highest result since 1978 and above the 2025 total.

business.title: Royal Mail staff say they were told to hide post to look like targets met
business.description: BBC Your Voice hears from postal workers who say "take the mail for ride" is a common phrase.

business.title: Warning issued over fresh wave of scam texts pretending to be from MyGoc
business.description: A﻿ustralians have been warned over a fresh wave of scam texts targeting anyone with a Medicare card.

business.title: UK must back North Sea oil and gas drilling, says trade body
business.description: The group says the country "urgently" needs to produce its own oil and gas to secure supplies.

business.title: GIFT Nifty up 410 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
business.description: Indian markets experienced a sharp sell-off on Monday, with the Nifty and Sensex plunging due to escalating Iran-Israel tensions, rising crude oil prices, and a record low for the rupee. Sentiment improved post-market after US President Trump postponed strikes on Iran, leading to a fall in oil prices and a positive Wall Street opening.

business.title: Stocks in news: HDFC Bank, IRB Infrastructure, Nitco, Indigo, Amber Enterprises, Sula Vineyard, Brand Concepts
business.description: Indian frontline indices closed sharply lower on Monday, with the Nifty and Sensex experiencing significant drops due to a sell-off in banking, auto, and consumer stocks. Escalating geopolitical tensions, rising crude oil prices, and a weakening rupee further impacted market sentiment.

business.title: Steel industry players blame China for excess capacity
business.description: Malaysian Iron and Steel Industry Federation says the surge in China’s steel exports has contributed to heightened competitive pressures across many regions, including Southeast Asia.

business.title: More than 5000 rooms are planned in these Sydney suburbs. None will count as a home
business.description: It was marketed as a solution to the rental crisis, but a Sydney council warns that the co-living housing “monoculture” is doing more harm than good.

business.title: Missing student found dead
business.description: THE search for a missing college student in India ended when her body was found in a drain, Malaysia Nanban reported.

science.title: CT Scans Solve Decades-Old Mystery of Triassic Cynodont
science.description: Known from a single skull discovered in South Africa in 1952, Cistecynodon parvus has been shuffled across the evolutionary tree: described at various times as a close relative of advanced cynodonts, a juvenile of another species and even something outside the group altogether.

science.title: Artemis II: Inside the Moon mission to fly humans further than ever
science.description: Find out about the astronauts and spacecraft involved in Nasa's Artemis II mission - the first time humans have flown to the Moon for fifty years.

science.title: Antarctic Ice Cycles Influence Distant Ocean Productivity
science.description: MADISON — Cycles in the growth and decay of Antarctica's ice sheets once shaped marine biological productivity thousands of miles away in the

science.title: UBC Unveils Tool to Halt Destructive Insect
science.description: Invasive species cost Canada billions of dollars each year. Now, a team led by UBC researchers has developed a new genomic test that can trace the

science.title: Global experiment supports Darwin's century-old hunch about auditory aesthetics
science.description: Charles Darwin suspected that humans and animals share similar aesthetic tastes. A new citizen science experiment supports this hypothesis, showing that people strongly prefer the exact same acoustic patterns that attract female animals.

science.title: Scientists observe atoms existing in two places at once
science.description: Quantum physicists at ANU have observed atoms entangled in motion. Their experiment using helium atoms represents a major advancement on similar experiments using photons, which are particles of light.
science.source: ANU College of Science and Medicine

science.title: Ancient Antarctic ice cycles impacted ocean productivity thousands of miles away
science.description: A new study by UW–Madison geoscientists, recently published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, found that the obliquity cycle — a 40,000-year astronomical cycle tied to changes in Earth’s axial tilt — influenced ocean productivity in subtropical latitudes about 34 million years ago when the Antarctic ice sheet was first expanding.

science.title: Prolonged Exposure To Microplastics Disrupts Metabolism Of Mediterranean Octocorals
science.description: Prolonged exposure to microplastics can disrupt vital physiological processes in gorgonians, such as respiration. Although these pollutants do not cause visible damage to tissues and cells, their effects could have an ecological impact on these organisms that structure the seabed, particularly if exposure continues over time occurs alongside other environmental pressures, such as ocean warming,...

ai.title: Early stage investors fight to stay competitive
ai.description: The rush to invest in AI startups has resulted in intense competition, with average funding size and valuation on the rise. Smaller venture funds face the challenge of getting squeezed out. In India, average investment at the seed level is at $3-5 million, with Series A upwards of 10 million, according to investors. That compares with non-AI startups’ average seed round of $2-4 million and Series A at $5-8 million.

ai.title: The SHARP generation: What to tell young people about AI and their careers
ai.description: Tips and tricks that will help propel you ahead in the AI race.

ai.title: Govt to rejig PLI 2.0 sops on high GPU prices, AI server tech
ai.description: The terms of the production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme for IT hardware need to be re-evaluated due to the rising cost of graphics processing units (GPUs) and the emergence of artificial intelligence (AI) servers, a senior government official said on Monday.

ai.title: World’s deadliest rat that can kill an elephant, and 8 other weird rodents that break every rule of nature
ai.description: Rodents are commonly regarded as pests, typically in the form of rats scavenging in rubbish bins or mice seeking refuge in corners. However, this is just the tip of the iceberg. Rodents have developed in ways which, across the globe, are not only unexpected but, in some cases, unbelievable. Rodents range from gliding animals in the forest to burrowing creatures with insect-like colonies. They are much more diverse than people think. While some are known for their bizarre looks, others are known for their extreme behaviours and abilities. One is even regarded as the most deadly rat, with enough poison to repel even the most formidable predators. They are incredible creatures, forcing us to think differently and showing us just how bizarre the world is.PC: AI-Generated

ai.title: Early investors fight to stay competitive
ai.description: Amid shrinking market shares early stage VCs look to niche areas to stay relevant in the AI race. ET explores the field.

ai.title: Hard Reset: India’s AI investment boom masks a deeper battle for survival
ai.description: Even as AI startups continue to raise top dollar, some are scrambling to rebuild business models and keep pace with the disruption, investors told ET.

ai.title: Indian GCC talent taking up global leadership positions at a faster clip
ai.description: Global capability centres (GCCs) in India are moving up the corporate ladder, with nearly 20% of such centres enjoying real strategic authority, up from just 5% a decade ago, according to data from ANSR and Deloitte India. The number of global leadership roles based in India has zoomed to over 6,500 from just 115 in 2015 and is poised to reach 30,000 by 2030 with AI expected to boost the trend, industry estimates suggest.

ai.title: Meta Advertising in 2026: Performance, Revenue and Platform Strategy
ai.description: Meta's $201 billion annual revenue is almost entirely driven by advertising. Here is how Meta's platform strategy and AI targeting tools are performing in 2026.
